name: Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (size undisclosed) served as 4 parallel specialized agent roles (coordinator 'Grok/Captain', research/fact-check 'Harper', math/logic 'Benjamin', creative/balance 'Lucas') sharing weights and KV-cache, debating before synthesizing one final answer
note: Multi-agent sibling of Grok 4.20, paralleling how 'Grok 4 Heavy' relates to 'Grok-4' elsewhere in this corpus: 4 specialized agent roles collaborate at inference time on every complex query, running as parallel replicas of the same underlying model rather than 4 independent models. xAI states the marginal inference cost is roughly 1.5-2.5x a single pass, not 4x. Public beta Feb 17, 2026; full API GA Mar 10, 2026 alongside the base model.
verificationNotes:
Sibling entry to 'Grok 4.20', confirmed via the same primary source: docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes explicitly announces both together ('Mar 10 -- Grok 4.20 and Grok 4.20 Multi-agent are live'), and I separately fetched the dedicated model card at docs.x.ai/developers/models/grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta-0309 directly (model slug `grok-4.20-multi-agent-0309`; aliases: grok-4.20-multi-agent, grok-4.20-multi-agent-latest, grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta-0309, grok-4.20-multi-agent-experimental-beta-0304). Same pricing as the base model ($1.25/$2.50 per 1M, $0.20 cached) and same stated 1,000,000-token context window, subject to the identical primary-vs-aggregator discrepancy documented on the sibling 'Grok 4.20' entry (independent aggregators including LLM Stats -- which separately tracks 'Grok-4.20 Multi-Agent Beta', released Mar 9, 2026, at $2.00/$6.00 and 2M context -- again disagree with the primary source's 1M/$1.25 figures, likely reflecting a later point-release; I trusted the live primary-source page). The 4-agent architecture and named roles (Grok/Captain, Harper, Benjamin, Lucas) are independently corroborated across multiple unrelated secondary write-ups (natural20.com citing NextBigFuture and a Yahoo Finance piece on Alpha Arena live-trading results; aimlapi.com; cometapi.com) describing the same four agents consistently enough to treat as accurate, even though xAI itself never published an official blog post for this release (confirmed absent: x.ai/news/grok-4-20 404s, and natural20.com explicitly notes xAI's last x.ai/news entry at the time was the Nov 2025 Grok 4.1 post). NO VERIFIED BENCHMARK SCORES for the same reasons documented on the sibling 'Grok 4.20' entry: no xAI-published benchmark table exists, LLM Stats shows 'Score pending' for this exact model, and I deliberately did not use the single-sourced Alpha Arena live-trading returns (+34.59%) or an 'estimated LMArena Elo of 1505-1535' cited by natural20.com, since neither is a standard benchmark in this corpus's approved slug taxonomy and the Elo figure is explicitly self-described as an estimate rather than a measured result. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): Final widened-methodology search for Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent (corpus/models/spacexai/grok-4-20-multi-agent.json) found NO citable benchmark result and confirms the conclusion should stand as "no public data exists." Not applicable for Chinese/Japanese-vendor search angle — this is xAI/SpaceXAI, an English-language vendor, so steps 1-2 of the brief don't apply. Checked per step 3/4:

- Read existing verificationNotes first: prior research already established no xAI-published benchmark table exists for this SKU, LLM Stats showed "Score pending," and an Alpha Arena live-trading return + a self-described "estimated" LMArena Elo (1505-1535) were deliberately excluded as non-standard/non-measured.
- Re-fetched llm-stats.com/models/grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta-0309 directly: still shows pricing/context only, zero benchmark scores.
- Fetched artificialanalysis.ai/providers/xai (full xAI model roster with Intelligence Index scores): no "Multi-Agent" SKU listed at all — only base Grok 4.20 0309 / 0309 v2 (reasoning and non-reasoning) appear. Confirmed AA tracks the base model only, not this variant.
- Fetched llm-stats.com/benchmarks/lmarena-text: only Grok-4.1 and Grok-4.1 Thinking listed for xAI; no Grok 4.20 of any kind, let alone the multi-agent SKU.
- Fetched openrouter.ai/x-ai/grok-4.20-multi-agent: pricing/context/provider info only, no benchmark scores.
- Fetched benchlm.ai/compare/grok-4-20-beta-vs-grok-4-20-multi-agent-beta (a dedicated aggregator comparison page): explicitly states "BenchLM does not have sourced benchmark coverage for Grok 4.20 Multi-agent yet," all 19 tested benchmark columns show "—" for this model, "0 shared benchmark results."
- Fetched apertis.ai/models/grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta: no benchmark scores present.
- Fetched aimlapi.com's Grok 4.20 review blog: confirmed all its reported numbers (Arena Elo ~1493, GPQA ~88%, HLE 50.7%) are attributed to "Grok 4.20" generically, with the multi-agent SKU only named as one of three release variants, no scores isolated to it.
- Web-searched combinations of "Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent" against GAIA, tau-bench, BFCL, function-calling/tool-use benchmarks (relevant given the model's agentic multi-agent design) — found general discussion of what these benchmarks measure but zero scores tied to this specific model.
- Checked for an xAI blog/Wayback angle: x.ai/news has no dedicated post (confirmed absent in both this and prior rounds); no distinct Wayback snapshot surfaced with different data.

Verdict: every number in circulation for "Grok 4.20" in mid-2026 web content is attributed to the base model, not the "-multi-agent-0309" SKU specifically, and the one aggregator (BenchLM) that explicitly tracks per-model sourced coverage confirms zero benchmark data exists for this variant. This looks like a genuine case of a vendor never publishing standard benchmark results broken out for a model configuration (the multi-agent inference mode is treated by both xAI and third parties as an inference-time architecture choice layered on the same base model, not a separately benchmarked release). Recommend closing out further benchmark-hunting for this entry unless a future xAI blog post or aggregator specifically isolates multi-agent-mode scores.
